key_insight: A single rollout can still provide useful token-level credit if a critic learns the prompt-prefix Pass@k success probability instead of relying on noisy multi-sample baselines.
engineering_relevance: This reduces sampling cost and improves credit assignment for RL training of language models, making reinforcement learning more practical for expensive reasoning tasks.
method: Train a calibrated token-level critic from Monte Carlo outcomes using one rollout per prompt.
Predict Pass@k at each prefix to score how likely the prefix can still lead to a successful completion.
Use the critic in SR-PPO to assign token-level advantages without comparing heterogeneous sampled traces.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports stable learning and consistent improvements in Pass@128 success on mathematical reasoning benchmarks including HMMT26 and AIME24.
watch_item: Pass@k-based credit is only as good as the critic calibration and may be sensitive to how rollout outcomes and prefixes are sampled.
paper_title: Learning with a Single Rollout via Monte Carlo Pass@k Critic
